pages.title: 明: environmental hints
pages.text: $(l)明$() reads the environment. Wood mostly reports darkness and a little danger. Stone starts reporting containers. Copper starts reporting ores. Iron and above gives more complete hints for danger, containers, ores, and traps.$(br2)It does not give exact wallhack coordinates. It is a clue, not a minimap.

pages.title: 脉: life sense
pages.text: $(l)脉$() senses nearby life, strength, and direction. Wood reaches 8 blocks and only gives rough movement. Copper reaches 16. Diamond reaches 24. Netherite reaches 28.$(br2)Better staves make the report closer to actual count, strength, and direction.

pages.title: 魄: death recovery
pages.text: $(l)魄$() can be written within 10 minutes after death. It pulls back part of the still-existing dropped items and experience from your death site.$(br2)It rolls item categories first, then rolls amounts within each category. Recovered items are removed from the death site, so it does not duplicate anything.

pages.title: Recovery ratio
pages.text: Wood recovers about 20% of item categories and 20% of each chosen category. Iron is about 50%. Diamond is about 70%. Netherite is about 80%.$(br2)For example, planks, a stone sword, and torches are grouped by item type first, then each selected group rolls its own amount.
